LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION TO SEAL RECORDS IS GRANTED IN PART; DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE IN PART. MOVING PARTY PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S (PUI) MOTION TO SEAL CERTAIN RECORDS FILED IN CONNECTION WITH PREVIOUS DISCOVERY MOTIONS (PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENA FILED AUGUST 13, 2013 AND PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH SUBPOENA FILED AUGUST 20, 2013) IS GRANTED. THE REQUIREMENTS OF RULES 2.550-2.551 DO NOT APPLY TO DISCOVERY MOTIONS. (RULE 2.550(A)(3).) A SPECIFIC ORDER MUST BE PREPARED BY MOVING PARTY IDENTIFYING THE DOCUMENTS THAT ARE TO BE FILED UNDER SEAL IN CONNECTION WITH THESE DISCOVERY MOTIONS. THE REMAINDER OF THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE MOVING PARTY HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN TO SEAL ANY OR ALL OF THESE DOCUMENTS. WITHOUT FURTHER SHOWING, INCLUDING A COPY OF THE SPECIFIC DOCUMENTS WHICH ARE THE SUBJECT OF THE SEALING REQUEST WITH THE SPECIFIC PORTIONS SOUGHT TO BE SEALED IDENTIFIED FOR THE COURT TO REVIEW, THE COURT CANNOT MAKE THE EXPRESS FACTUAL FINDINGS REQUIRED UNDER RULE 2.550(D). FURTHER, THE RECORDS HAVE ALREADY BEEN FILED IN THE PUBLIC RECORD. ANY FURTHER MOTION TO SEAL REQUIRES A REPRESENTATION FROM COUNSEL THAT IT DID NOT RECEIVE TIMELY NOTICE THAT CONFIDENTIAL DOCUMENTS WERE BEING LODGED WITH THE COURT, AS REQUIRED BY RULE 2.551(B)(3)(III). THE COURT CANNOT TELL FROM THE MOVING PAPERS WHETHER THE DOCUMENTS AT ISSUE WERE IDENTIFIED AS CONFIDENTIAL, AND WHETHER THE REQUIREMENTS OF RULE 2.551(B)(3)(III) WERE MET. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT (SEE ORDER FOR FULL DETAILS OF DOCUMENTS TO BE SEALED) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED AS TO EXHIBITS 1-7, TO THE EXTENT THAT THE MOTIONS WERE FILED, AND EXHIBITS 8-21 TO THE EXTENT THAT THE ARTICLES WERE PUBLISHED. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THE COURT CAN AMEND A JUDGMENT TO ADD A NON-PARTY ALTER EGO. (SEE, E.G. LEEK V. COOPER 194 CAL. APP. 4TH 399, 419.) FURTHER, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER A UNITY OF INTEREST EXISTS BETWEEN DEFENDANTS AND PUREG AND WHETHER AN INEQUITABLE RESULT WILL FOLLOW. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS' SEPARATE STATEMENT OF FACTS IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION ("PSS") 45-60; SEE ALSO EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS' SEPARATE STATEMENT OF ADDITIONAL MATERIAL FACTS ("AMF") 18-28.) AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANTS' EVER LOANED PUREG MONEY. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF PSS 62-63.) AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER THE ALLEGED TRANSFERS WERE FRAUDULENT UNDER CIV. CODE 3432. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF PSS 62-63.) AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SHIFT THE BURDEN BY PRODUCING PRIMA FACIE EVIDENCE THAT THE INSURANCE POLICY DID NOT EXIST. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS ("UMF") 62.) Off Calendar Jan-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARK MEYER, ESQ., 415.956.8400, FOR PLAINTIFF ROBERT CARTER.
JASON SCHEIDERER, ESQ., 816.460.2418, FOR Defendants Brookfield Asset Management Inc., Brookfield RPS, LLC, Real Living Real Estate, LLC And Brps LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: Defendants Brookfield Asset Management Inc., Brookfield RPS, LLC, Real Living Real Estate, LLC And Brps LLC'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted as to exhibits 1-7, to the extent that the motions were filed, and exhibits 8-21 to the extent that the articles were published. Plaintiffs' request for judicial notice is granted. As to the first cause of action, summary adjudication is denied. The Court can amend a judgment to add a non-party alter ego. (See, e.g. Leek v. Cooper 194 Cal. App. 4th 399, 419.) Further, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether a unity of interest exists between Defendants and PUREG and whether an inequitable result will follow. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Plaintiffs' Separate Statement of Facts in Support of Opposition ("PSS") 45-60; see also Evidence Cited in Support of Plaintiffs' Separate Statement of Additional Material Facts ("AMF") 18-28.) As to the fourth cause of action, summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendants' ever loaned PUREG money. (See Evidence Cited in Support of PSS 62-63.) As to the fifth cause of action, summary adjudication is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the alleged transfers were fraudulent under Civ. Code 3432. (See Evidence Cited in Support of PSS 62-63.) As to the third cause of action, summary adjudication is granted. Defendants shift the burden by producing prima facie evidence that the insurance policy did not exist. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 62.) Plaintiffs do not address the insurance policy in their Points and Authorities and do not produce any admissible evidence to controvert. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Mtn To Seal Records
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN L. FITZGERALD, TEL. NO. 689-1209 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PACIFIC UNION INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S Motion To Seal Records is Granted in part; denied without prejudice in part. Moving Party Pacific Union International, Inc.'s (PUI) motion to seal certain records filed in connection with previous discovery motions (Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Compliance With Subpoena filed August 13, 2013 and Plaintiffs' Motion to Compel Compliance With Subpoena filed August 20, 2013) is granted. The requirements of Rules 2.550-2.551 do not apply to discovery motions. (Rule 2.550(a)(3).) A specific order must be prepared by moving party identifying the documents that are to be filed under seal in connection with these discovery motions. The remainder of the motion is denied without prejudice. The moving party has not met its burden to seal any or all of these documents. Without further showing, including a copy of the specific documents which are the subject of the sealing request with the specific portions sought to be sealed identified for the Court to review, the Court cannot make the express factual findings required under Rule 2.550(d). Further, the records have already been filed in the public record. Any further motion to seal requires a representation from counsel that it did not receive timely notice that confidential documents were being lodged with the Court, as required by Rule 2.551(b)(3)(iii). The Court cannot tell from the moving papers whether the documents at issue were identified as confidential, and whether the requirements of Rule 2.551(b)(3)(iii) were met. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT (See Order for full details of documents to be sealed) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
